_comment: Single source of truth for the /screenshots/ page. The capture harness (demos/capture.sh) regenerates the frames listed here; build.mjs renders the page from this file. To add/change a surface: edit its entry, run the tape/render, re-run capture, rebuild. 'kind' drives layout: triptych=3-up, duo=2-up, single=1 centered, demo=animated video hero, gallery=grid of demo gifs, htmlframe=rendered web/email view.

hero.caption: MySQL → Postgres: a consistent snapshot, then live CDC with position, freshness, rows applied, and throughput.

sections.tagline: A continuous-sync stream as a live status panel — mode, position, freshness, rows applied, throughput, health, and recent events.

frames.caption: Snapshot done; streaming live changes, position advancing.
Rows applied and throughput climb as source changes arrive.
q / ctrl+c drains in-flight changes, then exits cleanly.

sections.tagline: A one-shot schema-and-data copy with a live phase checklist — tables, a per-table bulk-copy bar, then deferred indexes and constraints.

frames.caption: Tables created; bulk copy begins, indexes and constraints deferred.
The per-table bar reads 100%+ once InnoDB's row estimate is passed.
Summary panel: tables, rows, duration, and any warnings.

sections.tagline: Reconcile the target against the source, row-for-row (or by count), and report exactly what matched.

frames.caption: Schema compared, then the data comparison runs.
Tables checked / clean / mismatched / skipped.

sections.tagline: A chunked, optionally encrypted and signed logical backup — with an EndPosition so a later incremental resumes exactly where it left off.

frames.caption: Schema captured; rows stream out in size-bounded chunks.
Tables, rows, chunks, encrypted?, signed?, EndPosition.

sections.tagline: Replay a backup chain into a fresh target — schema, data, then constraints.

frames.caption: Schema restored; data replayed, constraints deferred.
Tables and rows restored.

sections.tagline: The fleet dashboard in your terminal — every stream's state, restarts, and time-in-state, with a detail pane for the selected stream.

frames.caption: Live fleet status; select any stream for its detail.

sections.tagline: The same fleet status as a browser dashboard, served on a local port — no terminal required.

frames.caption: The embedded HTML dashboard at --dashboard-listen.

sections.tagline: A scriptable per-stream health probe — freshness, position, lag, and spill — exit-code and JSON friendly for cron and CI.

frames.caption: Per-stream state, seconds-since-apply, lag, and spill.

sections.tagline: Live PlanetScale target metrics as a status panel — CPU, memory, storage, lag, and connections, sampled on a cadence, with threshold breaches surfaced as live events.

frames.caption: The live metrics panel; breaches appear in the events region.

sections.tagline: Threshold-breach alerts as a rendered HTML email (or Slack / webhook) — advisory, failure-isolated, never affecting the sync.

frames.caption: The HTML email template for a storage-utilisation breach.

sections.tagline: Prime the target's sequences and AUTO_INCREMENT columns past the migrated data so the app can write safely after the switch. Runs in milliseconds — here's the result panel.

frames.caption: Per-engine rollup: primed / noop / skipped / refused.

sections.tagline: Refresh the target's materialized views after a load or a sync catch-up.

frames.caption: Views refreshed / skipped.

sections.tagline: Install trigger-based CDC capture on a source with no native replication stream — reporting exactly what DDL it applied.

frames.caption: Statements applied, DDL-detection mode, server version.

sections.tagline: List the logical-replication slots on a Postgres source — the ACTIVE column shows at a glance which have a live consumer.

frames.caption: Name, plugin, active, WAL status, and LSNs — mirroring pg_replication_slots.

demos.title: Migrate in motion
demos.caption: A full MySQL → Postgres migration: phases, bulk-copy bar, summary.

demos.title: Encrypted backup → restore
demos.caption: Take an encrypted backup, then restore it into a fresh target — the safe roundtrip.

demos.title: Schema drift, refused
demos.caption: sluice catches a hand-edited target and refuses loudly with a per-column diff and copy-paste DDL.

demos.title: Schema change, forwarded
demos.caption: A column added to the source mid-sync is applied to the target automatically — the stream stays online, no drain and no recovery.
